fileName: MCLN EX48 CTRL + S Logging Trigger of Exiting Tasks.docx
webUrl: MCLN EX48 CTRL + S Logging Trigger of Exiting Tasks.docx
title: MCLN EX48 CTRL + S Logging Trigger of Exiting Tasks
fullText: Logging Trigger of Exited Picking Tasks Functionality McLane is seeing that users are accepting and entering into a task then immediately exiting the task through CTRL + X or CTRL + W . This results in the user getting assigned a new task and the original task goes to the bottom of the pile of available tasks. The reasoning behind exited tasks going to the end of the pile is to avoid unforeseen issues in the aisles, congestion, or any other reasons that a user has a real need to bypass the priority task assigned. WMOS is customized to log error message CUST 9989: "User: (USER123) exited task: (TASK123) of type (Task Type Name)” upon CTRL-X or CTRL-W functionality being invoked to detect when an INT50/51 picking task is exited by a user. This will help support and bring awareness to McLane Capstone Leadership by quantifying the number of times various users are exiting a picking task. The error message will be logged in both the database and the Message Logs UI and expected to display message: “USER: (USER123) exited task: (TASK123) of type (Task Type Name)”. No configuration is needed for the logging trigger. Once a user exits out of task, the logging trigger is hit and displayed in the Message Logs UI and in the database using below query: Select * from msg_log where MSG_ID = '9989' order by LOG_DATE_TIME DESC;

chunks.text:
[Document: MCLN EX48 CTRL + S Logging Trigger of Exiting Tasks]

Logging Trigger of Exited Picking Tasks Functionality McLane is seeing that users are accepting and entering into a task then immediately exiting the task through CTRL + X or CTRL + W . This results in the user getting assigned a new task and the original task goes to the bottom of the pile of available tasks. The reasoning behind exited tasks going to the end of the pile is to avoid unforeseen issues in the aisles, congestion, or any other reasons that a user has a real need to bypass the priority task assigned. WMOS is customized to log error message CUST 9989: "User: (USER123) exited task: (TASK123) of type (Task Type Name)” upon CTRL-X or CTRL-W functionality being invoked to detect when an INT50/51 picking task is exited by a user. This will help support and bring awareness to McLane Capstone Leadership by quantifying the number of times various users are exiting a picking task. The error message will be logged in both the database and the Message Logs UI and expected to display message: “USER: (USER123) exited task: (TASK123) of type (Task Type Name)”. No configuration is needed for the logging trigger. Once a user exits out of task, the logging trigger is hit and displayed in the Message Logs UI and in the database using below query: Select * from msg_log where MSG_ID = '9989' order by LOG_DATE_TIME DESC;
